record.field: OpenBao is an open source tool to store and manage secrets, certificates, keys, and other sensitive data — a community-driven fork of HashiCorp Vault.

OpenBao is an open source tool to store and manage secrets, certificates, keys, and other sensitive data — a community-driven fork of HashiCorp Vault.

Governed by the Linux Foundation, OpenBao provides a unified API, CLI, and web UI to centralize credentials across an organization while enforcing fine-grained, policy-based access and recording a detailed audit log. Beyond static key/value storage, it can issue short-lived dynamic credentials for databases and cloud providers, act as encryption-as-a-service, and run a built-in PKI to sign and rotate TLS certificates. It is a drop-in, community-governed alternative for teams that want to standardize secrets management without vendor lock-in.

Key features
  • Centralized, encrypted storage and lifecycle management for secrets, certificates, and keys
  • Dynamic, short-lived credentials generated on demand for databases and cloud platforms
  • Encryption-as-a-service (transit) to encrypt and decrypt data without exposing keys
  • Built-in PKI secrets engine to issue, sign, and rotate TLS certificates
  • Pluggable authentication methods with fine-grained, policy-based access control
  • Integrated Raft storage with snapshots and detailed audit logging for compliance
Best for

Platform, security, and DevOps teams that want a self-hosted, community-governed secrets manager to centralize credentials, PKI, and encryption across apps and infrastructure. Note: OpenBao ships sealed — after install you must run the one-time initialization and unseal flow and securely back up the unseal/recovery keys, since they cannot be recovered if lost.

services: openbao: image: openbao/openbao:latest container_name: '{{ INSTANCE_CODE }}' cap_add: - IPC_LOCK command: server -config=/etc/openbao/openbao.hcl environment: BAO_ADDR: http://127.0.0.1:8200 ports: - '{{ PORT_HTTP }}:8200' volumes: - ./openbao_data:/openbao/data - ./openbao.hcl:/etc/openbao/openbao.hcl:ro restart: unless-stopped networks: - internal - agz-proxy-network networks: internal: driver: bridge agz-proxy-network: external: true
